mission.title: operator-framework: Kubebuilder is not listed in prerequisites
mission.description: Kubebuilder is not listed in prerequisites . Requested by 9+ users.

steps.title: Check current operator-framework deployment
steps.description:
Verify your operator-framework version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n operator-framework -l app.kubernetes.io/name=operator-framework
helm list -n operator-framework 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working operator-framework installation.

steps.title: Review operator-framework configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant operator-framework configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n operator-framework -l app.kubernetes.io/name=operator-framework
kubectl get configmap -n operator-framework -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=operator-framework
```
Kubebuilder is Tightly coupled in requirements, which is fine but...
1. `kubebuilder` is not listed in [Prerequisites](https://sdk.operatorframework.io/docs/golang/quickstart/#prerequisites)
2.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Kubebuilder is not listed in prerequisites
steps.description:
- Add info in the quick start over how to setup the env test. Otherwise. it will fail when users run `docker-build`
- Add new doc with its options and more detailed information in the references.

- https://github.com/operator-framework/operator-sdk/issues/3461
```yaml
Failure [0.002 seconds]
[BeforeSuite] BeforeSuite 
/home/deepak/projects/go/src/operator/newoperator3/final/crda-operator/controllers/suite_test.go:52

  Unexpected error:
      <*fmt.wrapError | 0xc00046c2a0>: {
          msg: "failed to start the controlplane. retried 5 times: fork/exec /usr/local/kubebuilder/bin/etcd: no such file or directory",
          err: {
              Op: "fork/exec",
              Path: "/usr/local/kubebuilder/bin/etcd",
              Err: 0x2,
          },
      }
      failed to start the controlplane. retried 5 times: fork/exec /usr/local/kubebuilder/bin/etcd: n
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n operator-framework -l app.kubernetes.io/name=operator-framework
kubectl get events -n operator-framework --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Kubebuilder is not listed in prerequisites " is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
- Add info in the quick start over how to setup the env test. Otherwise. it will fail when users run `docker-build`
- Add new doc with its options and more detailed information in the references.

- https://github.com/operator-framework/operator-sdk/issues/3461
